quote: FEMM handles Magnetics, Electrostatics, Heat Flow, and Current Flow using finite element method with triangular mesh discretization of magnetic vector potential A

MuMax3 is a cross-platform tool designed for GPU-accelerated micromagnetic simulation program development. It utilizes NVIDIA hardware to deliver roughly a hundredfold speed increase over traditional processors, efficiently processing up to 16 million finite-difference grid points

The core algorithm relies on the Landau-Lifshitz micromagnetic formalism to compute magnetization evolution. It incorporates multiple physical models, including Heisenberg and RKKY exchange, Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction, Zhang-Li and Slonczewski spin-transfer torques, and both uniaxial and cubic anisotropy

ElmerFEM module includes an electromagnetic solver that handles time-harmonic and transient Maxwell equations in 2D and 3D. Elmer supports magnetostatic, eddy current, and full wave electromagnetic simulations

FEniCS is a computing platform for solving partial differential equations using the finite element method. Dolfinx is the next-generation FEniCS written in C++ with Python bindings. For electromagnetic simulation, FEniCS solves variational forms of Maxwell's equations using Python-defined weak formulations

magpylib is an open-source Python package designed to compute static magnetic fields generated by magnets, electric currents, and magnetic moments. The library employs analytical expressions that solve macroscopic magnetostatic problems. These mathematical solutions are implemented in a vectorized format to ensure rapid computation across multiple data points

Supported features include: Define arbitrary geometries for complex magnet shapes, Organize multiple items using collections for unified manipulation, Support for object trajectories and sensor orientation, Visualization through Matplotlib, Plotly, or Pyvista backends

Vimag Labs' VMSM eliminates traditional rotor magnets by relying on software and power electronics to generate its magnetic field instead. It generates and controls the rotor's magnetic field in real time using power electronics and proprietary control algorithms while maintaining a brushless, slip-ring-free design

The core patent covers 'A Robust Rotating Transformer Excited Synchronous Motor and Its Control.' The company targets industrial systems from 200 kW to 600 kW plus robotics, defense, and cooling applications. Vimag closed a $5 million Series A round led by Accel

ZF's inductive excitation motor design replaces permanent magnets and slip rings with a shaft-integrated transformer. The approach reduces losses by approximately 15% over conventional techniques while shrinking the motor's axial length by roughly 90 millimeters

VESC implements Field-oriented control (FOC) with Clarke and Park transformations, PID current controllers for d-q axis regulation, sensorless control modes using back-EMF observation and sliding mode observers

SpinMag's SynRM motors (SpinRel) eliminate permanent magnets entirely. SpinRel motors meet IE4 efficiency standards, with torque ranges from 39-92 Nm, peak power up to 31.0 kW, and winding voltages of 48-650 VDC. The rotor has no conductors, no windings, no permanent magnets, and no brushes
